description: This figure presents experimental and simulated microstructure evolutions in low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) during hot compression at 950°C under varying strain rate conditions. Panels (a) show EBSD-reconstructed microstructures and corresponding GOS maps at 0.9 strain, comparing constant strain rate (0.1 s⁻¹) with transient increases from 0.01 to 0.1 s⁻¹ at ε=0.2 and ε=0.4, revealing grain size increases from 28.89±3.09 μm to 37.16±2.26 μm and DRX fractions of 94.4-96.3%. Panel (b) compares microstructures at intermediate strains (0.4-0.5), showing larger grains (65.33±5.30 μm) under low strain rate versus smaller grains (50.53±5.62 μm) under high strain rate. Panels (c)-(e) present 3D cellular automaton simulations illustrating microstructure evolution under constant 0.1 s⁻¹ (c) and transient strain rate increases at ε=0.2 (d) and ε=0.4 (e), with color-coded grains indicating different deformation stages. The results demonstrate that delaying the strain rate transition to higher strains produces coarser final grains, validating the inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D CA model for multi-stage dynamic recrystallization.
